info.name: Project Management Platform API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Project Management Platform.

## Quick start
1. Import this collection and the companion environment file.
2. Set `baseUrl` (default `http://localhost:3000`) and `testPassword`.
3. Run **Auth → Register** then **Auth → Login** — the token is saved automatically.
4. Run **Projects → Create Project** — `projectId` is saved automatically.
5. Work through folders in order; most IDs are captured by test scripts.

## Variable chain
`accessToken` ← Login  
`projectId` ← Create Project  
`sprintId` ← Create Sprint  
`issueId`, `issueVersion` ← Create Issue  
`statusId` ← Get Board (first IN_PROGRESS column)  
`commentId` ← Create Comment  
`fieldId` ← Create Custom Field Definition  
`memberId` ← manually set to a second user's UUID for member tests

request.description: Create a new user account. `displayName` must be 2–100 chars, `password` min 8 chars.
Authenticates and saves `accessToken` to collection variables.

exec: // Auto-generate a unique key only if projectKey is empty, the bare 'DEMO'
// placeholder, or a previously auto-generated DEMO+5-digit key.
// A user-specified key (anything else) is preserved as-is.

request.description: Creates a project and saves `projectId`. The creator is automatically added as ADMIN. The key is generated uniquely per run (DEMO + last 5 digits of timestamp) to avoid 409 conflicts on repeated runs against the same server.

exec: pm.test('data is array with at least one member (owner)', () => {
console.warn('Skipping Add Member — set memberId variable to a registered user UUID first');

request.description: Requires ADMIN role. Set `memberId` variable to the UUID of a registered user before running. Valid roles: ADMIN, PROJECT_LEAD, MEMBER, VIEWER.

exec: console.warn('Skipping Remove Member — memberId not set');

request.description: Requires ADMIN role. Uses `memberId` variable (the user's UUID).
Requires PROJECT_LEAD role. Creates a sprint and saves `sprintId`.
Acquires MySQL advisory lock before transitioning. Returns 409 if another sprint is already active.

exec: pm.test('Sprint is COMPLETED and has velocity', () => {

request.description: Velocity is computed as sum of storyPoints on DONE issues. Unfinished issues can be moved to `nextSprintId`.

body.raw:
{
  "type": "STORY",
  "title": "As a user I can log in via email",
  "description": "Implement the login flow with JWT tokens",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "storyPoints": 5,
  "labels": ["auth", "backend"]
}

request.description: Saves `issueId` and `issueVersion` for subsequent PATCH/transition requests.
Returns cached board state. Saves `statusId` of the first IN_PROGRESS column for transitions.

exec: pm.expect(i.title).to.eql('Updated: As a user I can log in via email');

request.description: Requires `version` for optimistic locking. Returns 409 if version is stale.
Runs validation hooks (WIP limit, required fields). Returns 422 if blocked, 409 if transition is not allowed.
Subscribes the authenticated user; idempotent — re-watching does not error.
MentionParser extracts @usernames and resolves them to UUIDs. Triggers CommentAdded event → notification.
Only the original author can update. Returns 403 for other users.
Soft-delete. Only the original author can delete.
FULLTEXT search across issue titles and descriptions. Default `type=ISSUE`.
Searches comment content instead of issue titles. FULLTEXT index on comments.content.
Returns events from the domain event pipeline: CREATED, STATUS_CHANGED, COMMENTED, SPRINT_STARTED, SPRINT_COMPLETED.

item.name: 8. Custom Fields
item.description: Custom field definitions are scoped to a project (PROJECT_LEAD+ to mutate, VIEWER+ to read). Values are set per issue. Supported types: TEXT (max 5000 chars), NUMBER (finite), DROPDOWN (must match options), DATE (ISO 8601).

request.description: Requires VIEWER+ role. Returns definitions ordered by position, then createdAt.
Requires PROJECT_LEAD+ role. Saves `fieldId`. DROPDOWN requires `options` array. Other types: TEXT, NUMBER, DATE.
Requires PROJECT_LEAD+ role. Can update name, options, required, position. Type cannot be changed.
Returns all custom field values set on this issue, including the field definition via relation.
Upsert — creates or updates the value. Validates against field type (DROPDOWN: must be in options; NUMBER: must be finite; DATE: must be ISO 8601).

item.name: Set Field Value — invalid DROPDOWN value → 400

request.description: Removes the value for this field on this issue. The field definition is not affected.
Requires PROJECT_LEAD+ role. Cascades: deletes all values for this field across all issues. Run last so value operations above can use the field.
Always 200 if the Node process is alive (Kubernetes liveness probe).

exec: pm.test('Response has status and checks fields', () => {

request.description: Checks DB (SELECT 1) and Redis (PING). Returns `checks: {database, redis}` with ok/error per dependency.
Prometheus text exposition format. Scraped by your Prometheus instance.

item.name: 10. Cleanup
item.description: Run these LAST. Both requests destroy data that earlier sections depend on — running them earlier will cascade-delete sprints, issues, comments, and custom fields, causing all downstream requests to fail.

request.description: Deletes the issue and its comments, watchers, and custom field values. Run after all comment and custom-field tests.
Requires ADMIN role. Cascades to sprints, issues, comments, custom fields. Run last.
Uses a non-existent project UUID — user has no membership, RBAC returns 403.

item.name: Rate limiter — intentional burst (429 expected after 100 req/min)

request.description: Send this request 100+ times in under 60s to trigger the Redis sliding-window rate limiter. Use the Collection Runner with iterations=150.
